metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand the fallback payload 'at minimum' field list to include all fields required by schemas/export.schema.json: add schema_version (integer ≥ 1), ticket_id (a stable UUID or slug that will serve as source_id for idempotency), and status (e.g. 'open') alongside the existing title, ticket_type, description, and parent_id.
evidence: Plan: 'containing at minimum: title, ticket_type, description (including a ## Acceptance Criteria block), and parent_id if applicable'
src/rebar/schemas/export.schema.json: '"required": ["schema_version", "ticket_id", "ticket_type", "title", "status"]'
src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py pass 1 validation: 'if not sid or not rec.get("ticket_type"): continue' — a record without ticket_id is silently skipped, defeating idempotency-by-source_id entirely
docs/import-export.md: 'Idempotent by source_id' — source_id is derived from the imported ticket_id; a missing ticket_id means no idempotency key
findings.finding: The plan's fallback payload 'at minimum' field list (title, ticket_type, description, parent_id) is incomplete relative to schemas/export.schema.json's actual 'required' array, which additionally mandates schema_version, ticket_id, and status. A ChatGPT session following the plan's spec would produce a payload that fails rebar import's schema validation, breaking the entire AC6/AC7 fallback mechanism.
findings.location: Remediation / 'Fallback payload schema' subsection; AC6; AC7
scenarios: ChatGPT session produces a fallback payload with title/ticket_type/description/parent_id but no ticket_id, schema_version, or status — rebar import silently skips every record (they have no ticket_id), reports 0 created / 0 skipped, and the operator believes the import ran correctly
Payload has no schema_version — a future consumer that checks the discriminator field rejects the payload as malformed
Payload has no status — imported ticket lands in unknown state, violating the two-pass status reconstruction
findings.suggested_fix: Change the 'Fallback payload schema' clause to: 'containing at minimum: schema_version (integer, e.g. 1), ticket_id (a stable UUID/slug — this becomes the source_id for idempotency), ticket_type, title, status (e.g. "open"), description (including a ## Acceptance Criteria block), and parent_id if applicable — exactly matching the required fields of schemas/export.schema.json.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the `ticket_id` conflict in the fallback payload schema: either (a) specify that the agent should generate a stable UUID as the `ticket_id` / `source_id` for this payload (and document that this is a provenance id, not a rebar-assigned id), or (b) explicitly list the required fields (`ticket_id`, `schema_version`, `status`) in the AC6 minimum-field spec alongside `title`, `ticket_type`, and `description`, and clarify how the agent determines the `schema_version` integer.
evidence: src/rebar/schemas/export.schema.json line 9: "required": ["schema_version", "ticket_id", "ticket_type", "title", "status"]

The plan states: 'the reviewable ticket payload MUST conform to the rebar NDJSON export format (schemas/export.schema.json), containing at minimum: title, ticket_type, description … and parent_id if applicable'
The plan's own minimum-field list omits `ticket_id`, `status`, and `schema_version` — all required by the schema — while simultaneously mandating conformance to it
docs/import-export.md lines 57-68: import uses `ticket_id` from the payload as the `source_id` for idempotency; a payload lacking a real `ticket_id` cannot be deduped correctly on re-import
findings.finding: The fallback payload schema conformance requirement contains an unresolved capability conflict: the plan mandates the payload 'MUST conform to schemas/export.schema.json', but that schema's `required` array includes `ticket_id` — a field that does not exist for a ticket that has never been created. In a genuine fallback scenario (tracker worktree unavailable, native binary absent), there is no rebar-assigned `ticket_id` to populate, yet the plan instructs the agent to produce a document that would fail schema validation on this required field.
findings.location: Remediation section — 'Fallback payload schema (resolves T8 finding on AC6)' and AC6
scenarios: A ChatGPT session operating in fallback mode constructs a payload it calls 'schema-conformant' but lacks a ticket_id; a maintainer attempting `rebar import` on it gets a validation or idempotency failure

The guidance author interprets 'ticket_id' as something the agent should invent (e.g., a UUID), which would conflict with the import path's assumption that ticket_id is a stable, source-system identifier for idempotency
findings.suggested_fix: Augment the fallback payload spec to state: the agent generates a stable UUID as the `ticket_id` field (this becomes the `source_id` for `rebar import` idempotency), sets `status` to `open`, and uses the `schema_version` integer from the most recent `rebar export` output or from `schemas/export.schema.json`'s documented current version. Add these three fields to the AC6 minimum-field list.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand the fallback payload minimum-field spec (AC6 and Exceptional import mechanism) to include the fields rebar import actually requires: ticket_id (a stable UUID the ChatGPT session generates, used as source_id for idempotency), status (e.g. 'open'), schema_version (integer), priority, and tags — matching the required fields in schemas/export.schema.json and ticket_state.schema.json.
evidence: Plan states: 'the reviewable ticket payload MUST conform to the rebar NDJSON export format (schemas/export.schema.json), containing at minimum: title, ticket_type, description (including a ## Acceptance Criteria block), and parent_id if applicable'
schemas/export.schema.json line 9: 'required': ['schema_version', 'ticket_id', 'ticket_type', 'title', 'status']
ticket_state.schema.json line 8: 'required': ['ticket_id', 'ticket_type', 'title', 'status', 'priority', 'tags'] (inherited via allOf)
import_ndjson.py line 223: 'if not sid or not rec.get("ticket_type"): warn(...) continue' — record without ticket_id is silently skipped, never imported
import_ndjson.py line 108-115: _rec_sid() uses rec.get('ticket_id') as the source_id key for idempotency; missing ticket_id means idempotency dedupliation cannot work
Evidence class: Verified (observed against schema file and source code)
findings.finding: The plan's 'fallback payload schema' (AC6 and the Exceptional import mechanism section) specifies minimum fields of title, ticket_type, description, and parent_id, but schemas/export.schema.json requires schema_version, ticket_id, status, priority, and tags in addition; the rebar import implementation (import_ndjson.py line 223) silently skips any record missing ticket_id. A fallback payload conforming only to the plan-enumerated minimum fields cannot be successfully ingested via rebar import.
findings.location: Remediation > Fallback payload schema (resolves T8 finding on AC6) / AC6 / Exceptional import mechanism section
scenarios: A maintainer receives a fallback payload from a ChatGPT session containing only title, ticket_type, description, parent_id and runs rebar import on it: the importer silently skips every record (no ticket_id), emitting only warnings, with created=0 and no error exit code — giving a false impression of success or leaving the operator confused.

The idempotency guarantee (skip-by-source_id) fails because source_id is derived from ticket_id; without ticket_id the deduplication scan finds nothing to match, and any re-run cannot detect prior partial attempts.
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Fallback payload schema' section, replace 'containing at minimum: title, ticket_type, description …, and parent_id' with 'containing at minimum: ticket_id (a freshly generated UUID, used as source_id for idempotency), schema_version (integer, currently 1), ticket_type, title, status ("open"), priority (integer 0–4), tags (empty array []), description (including an ## Acceptance Criteria block), and parent_id if applicable'. Add a note that ticket_id is mandatory for rebar import to ingest the record and for the idempotency mechanism to function.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the capability-check section (AC2/AC6) to specify a safe, explicit agent action for each partial-failure combination that does NOT trigger the defined fallback — at minimum, state whether the agent should surface a capability-gap report, escalate to the operator, or treat the partial failure as equivalent to full fallback.
evidence: 'Any other combination of capability failures does not trigger the fallback; the guidance must state this rule explicitly so an executor does not need to infer it.'
'Native ticket creation is impossible' is defined as tracker-worktree fails AND network/binary fails — the rule is silent on what to do when exactly one of those two conditions holds.
AC2 asks for a check covering six dimensions but does not require the guidance to specify agent behavior for every non-triggering partial-failure combination.
findings.finding: The capability-check decision rule specifies exactly one combination that triggers the fallback (tracker-worktree fails AND network/binary fails), and states all other combinations do NOT trigger the fallback — but provides no guidance on what the agent should do in partial-failure states that don't meet the fallback trigger yet still prevent native ticket creation (e.g., worktree accessible but rebar binary absent; binary present but worktree stale/corrupt; Gerrit unavailable but tracker worktree reachable). In those states the agent is told 'do not fall back' but is given no alternative action, leaving it with no valid exit path.
findings.location: Remediation — 'Capability check and decision rule' paragraph; AC2 and AC6
scenarios: A ChatGPT session finds the tracker worktree checkout present and valid but the rebar binary (and MCP server) unavailable: the fallback trigger is not met (worktree dimension passes), yet native CLI creation is impossible — the guidance gives the agent no instruction for this state, so it either stalls or improvises.

A session finds the binary available but the worktree checkout stale (HEAD diverged, rebar exists returns an error): again the trigger is not met, native creation fails, and there is no documented recovery action.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a small decision table under the capability-check rule: for each partial-failure combination (e.g., worktree OK but binary absent; binary OK but worktree stale), name the agent action — e.g., 'report the specific missing dimension and block until the operator resolves it; do not attempt ticket creation and do not produce a fallback payload claiming the worktree is unreachable.' This prevents the agent from improvising an unsafe path while keeping the precise fallback trigger unchanged.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define agent behavior for each non-triggering partial-failure combination in the capability-check decision rule: at minimum, specify what the agent should do when the tracker-worktree is present but the binary/MCP is unavailable (and vice versa), so no capability-check outcome leaves the agent without a sanctioned next action.
evidence: Plan text: 'Any other combination of capability failures does not trigger the fallback; the guidance must state this rule explicitly so an executor does not need to infer it.'

Plan text: 'Native ticket creation is impossible is defined precisely as: the tracker-worktree dimension fails AND the network/remote dimension fails or the native rebar/rebar-mcp binary is unavailable'
No plan text specifies what an agent should do when, e.g., worktree is accessible but binary is absent (fallback not triggered, but native creation still impossible)
AC2 requires documenting all six dimensions but does not require documenting agent behavior for each partial-failure combination
findings.finding: The capability-check decision rule defines exactly ONE triggering combination for the fallback (tracker-worktree fails AND network/binary fails) and states all other combinations do NOT trigger it — but provides no specified agent action for partial-failure states that still prevent native ticket creation (e.g., worktree reachable but rebar binary absent; binary present but worktree stale/unresponsive). An agent in such a state is explicitly told 'don't fall back' but given no sanctioned alternative, leaving it with no valid exit path.
findings.location: Remediation — 'Capability check and decision rule' paragraph; AC2; AC6
scenarios: A ChatGPT session detects the tracker worktree checkout is present (rebar exists returns 0) but the rebar CLI binary is not on PATH: fallback trigger not met (worktree passes), yet native creation is impossible — the agent has no sanctioned action.
A session finds the binary available but the worktree HEAD diverged and rebar exists fails with a store error: not meeting the strict trigger, yet creation fails — no documented recovery path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the AC2 proving command to grep for all six capability dimensions (e.g. add `&& grep -qi 'local checkout' ... && grep -qi 'github connector' ... && grep -qi 'network' ... && grep -qi 'rebar-mcp'`).
evidence: "Verify: grep -qi 'tracker worktree' docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md && grep -qi 'gerrit' docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md"
AC2 text requires all six dimensions: local checkout, native rebar/rebar-mcp, tracker worktree, network/remote access, GitHub connector, Gerrit availability
findings.finding: AC2's grep proving command checks only two of the six required capability dimensions ('tracker worktree' and 'gerrit'), leaving local checkout, native rebar/rebar-mcp binary, network/remote access, and GitHub connector unverified by any proving command.
scenarios: A guide that mentions 'tracker worktree' and 'gerrit' but omits 'local checkout' or 'GitHub connector' passes AC2 as written yet fails the stated requirement.
findings.suggested_fix: Verify: grep -qi 'tracker worktree' docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md && grep -qi 'gerrit' docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md && grep -qi 'local checkout' docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md && grep -qi 'github connector' docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md && grep -qi 'rebar-mcp' docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md && grep -qi 'network' docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add grep checks for the remaining AC7 sub-requirements: `grep -qi 'ndjson' docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md && grep -qi 'branch' docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md && grep -qi 'contents.api\|contents api' docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md && grep -qi 'hlc\|timestamp' docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md`.
evidence: "Verify: grep -qi 'rebar import' docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md && grep -qi 'rebar validate\|rebar fsck' docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md"

AC7 text enumerates: 'accepts NDJSON (not raw events)', 'assigns fresh local ids/HLC timestamps', 'requires confirming the target branch head before invocation', 'never raw event-file injection or Contents API writes'
findings.finding: AC7's grep proving command checks that 'rebar import' and 'rebar validate'/'rebar fsck' appear, but does not verify the other required statements: that the path accepts NDJSON (not raw events), assigns fresh local ids/HLC timestamps, requires confirming the target branch head before invocation, and prohibits raw event-file injection and Contents API writes.
scenarios: A guide that simply says 'use rebar import and run rebar validate afterward' passes AC7's grep while omitting branch-confirmation, NDJSON specificity, and the explicit prohibition on Contents API writes.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit failure-handling instructions to the exceptional import mechanism: specify (a) what to do when `rebar import` exits non-zero (surface error verbatim, retain the NDJSON payload, report failure without claiming persistence, do not retry automatically), and (b) what to do when `rebar validate`/`rebar fsck` reports errors after a successful import (escalate to maintainer, halt further imports until resolved).
evidence: Plan text: 'the operator runs rebar validate (or rebar fsck) against the tracker to confirm store integrity' — no failure action defined
Plan text: 'the only sanctioned exceptional import path is rebar import … never raw event-file injection and never a GitHub Contents API write' — no retry, rollback, or error-report instruction
AC7 verify clause: grep -qi 'rebar import' ... && grep -qi 'rebar validate|rebar fsck' — checks keyword presence only, not failure-handling text
Cached prior finding (2d86cf3c) independently surfaced this same gap
findings.finding: The plan prescribes `rebar import` as the exceptional import path (AC7) and requires `rebar validate`/`rebar fsck` afterward, but neither the Remediation nor AC7 specifies what the agent should do when `rebar import` exits non-zero or when post-import validation reports errors — leaving a complete gap in the error/recovery protocol for this LLM-facing sub-step.
scenarios: rebar import exits non-zero due to lock contention or malformed NDJSON — agent has no prescribed response and may incorrectly claim persistence succeeded or attempt a raw fallback write.
rebar validate reports integrity errors post-import — guidance is silent on whether to roll back, escalate, or retry, leaving agents to improvise.
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Exceptional import mechanism' paragraph, add: 'If `rebar import` exits non-zero, surface the full stderr output, retain the NDJSON payload unchanged, and report that ticket creation did not occur. Do not attempt raw event-file writes as a fallback. If `rebar validate` or `rebar fsck` reports errors after a successful import, halt and escalate to a maintainer — do not invoke `rebar import` again until errors are resolved.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a concrete, known-id-independent probe for the tracker-worktree dimension — for example, checking for the existence of the `.tickets-tracker/` directory and a `tickets` branch checkout within it, or running `rebar list --no-pull` as an existence probe — so the dimension check is evaluable on first contact without a pre-known ticket id.
evidence: Plan states: 'tracker-worktree (a directory containing a valid tickets branch checkout, confirmed via rebar exists <known-id> or equivalent)'
docs/cli-reference.md line 108: 'Usage: rebar exists <ticket_id|alias>   (exit 0=exists, 1=not found)'
A session that lacks the native binary cannot run `rebar exists`; a session that has the binary but no known-id cannot validate the worktree with confidence
ABSENCE: no specification of a fallback probe (e.g., checking for the `.tickets-tracker/` directory directly, or using `rebar list` with --no-pull) when no known-id is available
findings.finding: The tracker-worktree dimension check uses `rebar exists <known-id>` as the verification probe, but the plan does not specify how a connector-limited ChatGPT session (which by definition may lack native CLI access) obtains a 'known-id' to probe against — creating a circular dependency in the capability-check protocol itself.
findings.location: Remediation section — 'Capability check and decision rule' and AC2
scenarios: A ChatGPT session's first interaction with a project has no known ticket ids; it cannot run `rebar exists` meaningfully and cannot distinguish a healthy empty store from a missing worktree
The GitHub connector reveals a file tree but not the tickets worktree (a separate git worktree), so the connector itself cannot supply a known-id to probe

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in AC8 the concrete forbidden-phrase assertions the test must make (e.g. `assert 'github issues' not in content.lower()` and `assert 'contents api' not in content.lower() or 'not' in ...`) so an executor can implement the test without inferring its logic.
evidence: "A lightweight documentation test verifies that ChatGPT guidance remains linked from AGENTS.md and does not contradict canonical Rebar or Gerrit rules (e.g. does not claim GitHub Issues or direct Contents-API writes are a sanctioned path)."
The verify command is only `tests/unit/test_chatgpt_guide_links.py exits 0`, with no statement of what assertions the test must contain.
findings.finding: AC8 requires the test to verify that the guide 'does not contradict canonical Rebar or Gerrit rules', but the plan provides no specification of what the test should actually check to satisfy that clause — leaving 'does not contradict' as a subjective/effort-framed obligation an automated test cannot evaluate without further definition.
scenarios: An executor writing the test has no actionable specification for the 'does not contradict' clause and must invent the assertion logic, producing untestable or vacuous checks.
A test that only asserts the file exists and AGENTS.md mentions ChatGPT will exit 0 while entirely skipping the contradiction check.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC8: 'The test must assert (a) AGENTS.md contains a hyperlink or path reference matching `docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md`; (b) the guide file does not contain the phrases "GitHub Issues" used as a tracker substitute, nor "Contents API" used as a sanctioned write path.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen the AC1 verify command to confirm an actual file reference exists, e.g. `grep -qi 'chatgpt-agent-guide' AGENTS.md`.
evidence: "Verify: grep -qi 'chatgpt' AGENTS.md"
AC1 outcome: 'discoverable from AGENTS.md without bloating the canonical startup context'
findings.finding: AC1's proving command (`grep -qi 'chatgpt' AGENTS.md`) verifies only that the string 'chatgpt' appears somewhere in AGENTS.md, not that there is an actual navigable link or reference to `docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md`; a comment, a warning, or any stray mention satisfies the grep while leaving the guide undiscoverable.
scenarios: A line in AGENTS.md saying 'Note: ChatGPT sessions may have limitations' passes the grep but provides no path to the new guidance file.
findings.suggested_fix: Verify: grep -qi 'chatgpt-agent-guide' AGENTS.md

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit failure-handling instructions to the exceptional import guidance: specify what to do when `rebar import` exits non-zero (e.g., surface the error verbatim, do not claim persistence, retain the NDJSON payload for retry) and when `rebar validate`/`rebar fsck` reports errors (e.g., escalate to maintainer, do not invoke import again until errors are resolved).
evidence: 'the operator runs `rebar validate` (or `rebar fsck`) against the tracker to confirm store integrity' — no failure action defined
'the only sanctioned exceptional import path is `rebar import`' — no retry, rollback, or error-report instruction
AC7 verify clause only checks that the keywords appear, not that failure handling is documented
findings.finding: The guidance prescribes `rebar import` as a write operation (AC7) and requires `rebar validate`/`rebar fsck` afterward, but does not specify what the agent should do when `rebar import` exits non-zero or when post-import validation reports errors — leaving agents with no defined error-surfacing or recovery behavior for this failure path.
scenarios: rebar import exits non-zero due to a lock contention or malformed NDJSON — agent/operator has no prescribed response
rebar validate reports integrity errors after import — guidance is silent on whether to roll back, re-attempt, or escalate
findings.suggested_fix: In the `rebar import` exceptional-path section, add a paragraph: 'If `rebar import` exits non-zero, surface the full error output, retain the NDJSON payload, and report that import failed without claiming ticket creation. Do not retry automatically. If `rebar validate` or `rebar fsck` reports errors after a successful import exit, stop and escalate to the repository maintainer before any further tracker writes.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either (a) define a stricter 'fallback-payload' schema/subsection in `src/rebar/schemas/` that marks `description` and `parent_id` as required for ChatGPT-generated payloads, or (b) explicitly state in the guidance that the fallback payload applies ADDITIONAL constraints beyond the base schema's required fields and enumerate them inline so an agent reading the guide has a complete, self-contained rule.
evidence: Plan text: 'containing at minimum: title, ticket_type, description (including a ## Acceptance Criteria block), and parent_id if applicable'
src/rebar/schemas/export.schema.json required array: [schema_version, ticket_id, ticket_type, title, status] — description and parent_id are absent from required
src/rebar/schemas/ticket_state.schema.json: 'description: { type: [string, null] }' and 'parent_id: { type: [string, null] }' — both nullable/optional
findings.finding: The plan requires the fallback payload to contain 'at minimum: title, ticket_type, description (including a ## Acceptance Criteria block), and parent_id if applicable' — but `src/rebar/schemas/export.schema.json` only marks `schema_version`, `ticket_id`, `ticket_type`, `title`, and `status` as `required`; `description` and `parent_id` are optional in the schema. The processing rule (what the agent MUST include) is not co-located with or reflected in the referenced schema, so an agent reading the schema alone cannot infer the plan's stricter requirement.
scenarios: A ChatGPT agent constructs a conforming-to-schema fallback payload that omits description and parent_id (both optional per schema), then an operator cannot import it meaningfully via rebar import because the ticket lacks an AC block.
An implementer writing the guide copies the schema's required fields without noting the plan's stricter minimum, producing incomplete guidance.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `tests/unit/test_codex_agent_guidance.py` as a potentially affected consumer of the AGENTS.md edit and confirm the planned insertion point does not displace the `**Codex Gerrit workflow rule:**` … `## Record your work` landmarks that test parses.
evidence: tests/unit/test_codex_agent_guidance.py:10-11: `start = text.index("**Codex Gerrit workflow rule:")` / `end = text.index("\n\n## Record your work", start)` — the test slices AGENTS.md between these two landmarks
Plan AC1: 'A ChatGPT-specific guidance file or clearly labeled section is discoverable from AGENTS.md without bloating the canonical startup context. Verify: `grep -qi chatgpt AGENTS.md`'
test_codex_agent_guidance.py is not mentioned anywhere in the plan's edit targets or acknowledged consumer list
findings.finding: tests/unit/test_codex_agent_guidance.py is an unenumerated consumer of AGENTS.md that structurally validates specific AGENTS.md content (parses between section landmarks). The plan modifies AGENTS.md (AC1 requires adding a ChatGPT guidance link discoverable via grep). If the modification inserts content before the '## Record your work' section header or alters nearby structure, the existing test's string-index parsing could fail or silently validate the wrong range.
scenarios: Implementer adds a new ## ChatGPT Agent Guide section before '## Record your work' in AGENTS.md, inadvertently shifting the region that test_codex_agent_guidance.py parses, causing that test to silently cover wrong content or raise ValueError on text.index().
findings.suggested_fix: Add `tests/unit/test_codex_agent_guidance.py` to the plan's 'files to check / not break' list and specify that the ChatGPT guidance link will be added as a pointer line within an existing section (e.g. appended to the 'Codex environment rule' block or placed after `## Record your work`) rather than as a new top-level section inserted between the two landmarks.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan (e.g. add a cross-reference in AC5 or AC7) that AC5 covers the *general* append-only prohibition and AC7 covers the *import-specific* prohibition, so the guidance file is required to address both contexts explicitly rather than satisfying both ACs with a single overlapping statement.
evidence: AC5: 'Guidance prohibits hand-authoring, modifying, or deleting event files and explains the append-only reason.'
AC7: '…names rebar import … as the only sanctioned path — never raw event-file injection or Contents API writes…'
Both address the same prohibited action (raw event-file writing) but in different contexts without a cross-reference.
findings.finding: AC5 and AC7 partially overlap in scope: AC5 prohibits 'hand-authoring, modifying, or deleting event files' and requires explaining the append-only reason, while AC7 also states 'never raw event-file injection'. The boundary between these two criteria is not crisp — an implementer could satisfy AC5's grep ('append-only') without explicitly calling out raw event-file injection as the forbidden act in the import context, and vice versa. This near-duplication risks both criteria being satisfied superficially by the same sentence.
scenarios: An implementer writes one sentence satisfying both greps and both ACs, leaving the guide ambiguous about whether the AC5 general prohibition and the AC7 import-specific prohibition are the same rule or different rules.
findings.suggested_fix: In AC5, add: '(general prohibition; see AC7 for the import-specific corollary)'. In AC7, add: '(specific application of AC5's append-only rule to the exceptional import path)'. This makes the non-duplication intent explicit.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a note in `docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md` (and optionally a cross-reference in AGENTS.md) stating that the capability-check dimension list must be updated whenever Rebar's CLI/MCP surface or connector set changes, and name the section/file an author should update (e.g., 'If a new remote MCP endpoint is added, revise the capability-check table in this section and update the decision rule accordingly').
evidence: The decision rule enumerates exactly six dimensions and defines 'impossible' precisely — but this enumeration is only in prose
No AGENTS.md note, ADR, or config entry references where to update the capability list if Rebar gains new capabilities
The rule is a threshold/business-rule that the plan itself flags as resolving T8 findings, suggesting it was already contested and is likely to evolve
findings.finding: The 6-dimension capability check is defined as a prose decision rule in the guidance doc with no note about where to update it when Rebar's toolset or connector set evolves (e.g., a future remote MCP endpoint or a new connector type); this makes the rule hard to maintain and easy to silently diverge from actual Rebar capabilities.
findings.location: Remediation / 'Capability check and decision rule' subsection; AC2
scenarios: A new rebar-mcp remote endpoint is added that allows ticket creation without a local tracker worktree — guidance still prohibits it because the six-dimension rule is unchanged
A reviewer updates `docs/import-export.md` with a new import variant but forgets to update the capability rule in the guidance doc

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the guidance that the capability check is a best-effort snapshot and that any runtime failure of a `rebar` or `rebar-mcp` invocation (even after a passing capability check) must trigger the same fallback as a failed capability check: produce the NDJSON payload, report the exact error, and do not claim persistence.
evidence: 'Native ticket creation is impossible is defined precisely as: the tracker-worktree dimension fails AND the network/remote dimension fails or the native rebar/rebar-mcp binary is unavailable' — this is evaluated once at decision time
No guidance on handling capability loss after the decision is made
The locked write path surfaces errors, but the guidance does not tell the agent to re-evaluate and produce the fallback payload on a mid-operation failure
findings.finding: The capability check is a check-then-act sequence: the agent confirms tracker worktree availability, then proceeds to invoke `rebar` or `rebar import`. If the worktree or binary becomes unavailable between the check and the act (TOCTOU), the guidance provides no fallback — the agent has already decided native creation is possible and may not produce the fallback payload.
findings.location: Remediation / 'Capability check and decision rule' subsection; AC6, AC7
scenarios: Agent checks tracker worktree → confirms available → worktree becomes unmounted mid-session → rebar invocation fails → agent has no instruction to fall back to the NDJSON payload path
Concurrent process removes the rebar binary between capability check and invocation
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence: 'If any `rebar` or `rebar-mcp` invocation fails at runtime — regardless of the outcome of the initial capability check — treat it as equivalent to a failed capability check: produce the fallback NDJSON payload, surface the exact error message, and report that no ticket was persisted.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that `docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md` must be added to `docs/README.md` (the existing `test_current_repo_index_is_clean` gate already enforces this), and scope `tests/unit/test_chatgpt_guide_links.py` to only the genuinely new invariants not covered by existing tests: (a) AGENTS.md cross-reference, and (b) content invariants (no GitHub Issues / no Contents-API writes as sanctioned path) — following the established pattern in `test_codex_agent_guidance.py`.
evidence: tests/unit/test_check_docs_index.py:44: `def test_current_repo_index_is_clean(): assert chk.main(['--check']) == 0` — runs against the live repo, catches any `docs/*.md` not linked from `docs/README.md`
tests/unit/test_codex_agent_guidance.py:1-20: establishes exact phrase-assertion pattern for agent guidance files; AC8 test would be a structural twin
scripts/check_docs_index.py:36-61: `find_unindexed` already enforces docs/README.md completeness for every living top-level docs/*.md

AC8 does not mention adding `docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md` to `docs/README.md`, which would trigger the existing gate and make a separate docs-linkage assertion in the new test redundant for that dimension
findings.finding: AC8 proposes `tests/unit/test_chatgpt_guide_links.py` to verify that `docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md` is linked from `AGENTS.md` and does not contradict canonical rules. However, the codebase already has `tests/unit/test_check_docs_index.py::test_current_repo_index_is_clean` (which enforces every `docs/*.md` is linked from `docs/README.md`) and `tests/unit/test_codex_agent_guidance.py` (which establishes the exact pattern of phrase-asserting a guidance section). The docs-index-linking portion of the proposed test duplicates an invariant already enforced by the existing gate — though the AGENTS.md-linkage assertion and the content-invariant assertions are genuinely new. The plan does not acknowledge this overlap or explain why the new test cannot fold its docs-linkage concern into the existing infrastructure.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC8: 'A lightweight documentation test … tests/unit/test_chatgpt_guide_links.py'
scenarios: A reviewer implementing AC8 writes a test that re-checks `docs/README.md` linkage — a concern already caught by the existing docs-index gate — while missing that the same file also needs a `docs/README.md` entry to satisfy the existing gate.
Maintenance cost: two tests now guard overlapping invariants; when the guidance file moves or is renamed, both tests must be updated.
findings.suggested_fix: In AC1, add: 'and that `docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md` is listed in `docs/README.md` (required by the existing `test_current_repo_index_is_clean` gate).' In AC8, narrow the new test's scope to: (a) `assert 'chatgpt' in AGENTS.read_text().lower()` and (b) content-invariant phrase assertions on `docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md` (no GitHub Issues, no Contents-API writes); drop any docs-index-linkage assertion that duplicates the existing gate.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct every citation of `schemas/export.schema.json` in the plan (Remediation, AC6, AC7) to the actual repository path `src/rebar/schemas/export.schema.json`.
evidence: Plan text: 'fallback payload MUST conform to the rebar NDJSON export format (schemas/export.schema.json)'
Plan text (AC6): 'grep -qi export.schema.json docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md'
list_directory('.') — no 'schemas/' directory at repo root
search_files('.', 'export.schema.json') returns only 'src/rebar/schemas/export.schema.json:3' and doc/CHANGELOG references — no root-level schemas/
Actual file: src/rebar/schemas/export.schema.json (confirmed readable)
findings.finding: The plan cites 'schemas/export.schema.json' (implying a root-level path) in the Remediation section, AC6, and AC7, but the actual file lives at 'src/rebar/schemas/export.schema.json'. A root-level 'schemas/' directory does not exist in the repository. Implementers following the plan to locate, reference, or link to this schema will use an incorrect path, and any generated guidance that hardcodes 'schemas/export.schema.json' without qualification will be wrong.
findings.location: Remediation section ('schemas/export.schema.json' citation); AC6 verify step; AC7 verify step
scenarios: An implementer authoring docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md follows the plan's path literally and writes 'schemas/export.schema.json', producing broken guidance that points agents to a non-existent path.
A documentation test that tries to resolve or validate the schema reference fails because the root-level path does not exist.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace every occurrence of `schemas/export.schema.json` in the plan text with `src/rebar/schemas/export.schema.json` (the verified location of the file in the repository).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `schemas/export.schema.json` in the plan is an intentional short-form reference (matching the convention in docs/import-export.md) or should be corrected to `src/rebar/schemas/export.schema.json`, and make the guidance file consistent.
evidence: Plan: 'the reviewable ticket payload MUST conform to the rebar NDJSON export format (`schemas/export.schema.json`)'
Actual file path confirmed by search: `src/rebar/schemas/export.schema.json`
docs/import-export.md:24 references it as `schemas/export.schema.json` (also short-form), so the convention may be intentional — but the plan does not clarify this
findings.finding: The plan consistently refers to the export schema as `schemas/export.schema.json` (without a directory prefix), but the file actually lives at `src/rebar/schemas/export.schema.json`. Guidance authors following the plan literally could document an incorrect path in docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md, and reviewers grepping for `export.schema.json` (AC6 verify) would still pass regardless of the path prefix used.
scenarios: An implementer writes `schemas/export.schema.json` as a clickable path in docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md; a reader following the link from a repo checkout finds the file is not at that path, reducing trust in the guidance.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the 'Exceptional import mechanism' section that `git fetch + rebar exists <known-id>` is a best-effort freshness check (not a safety invariant) and explicitly state that the real safety guarantees come from `rebar import`'s write lock (I5) and idempotency-by-`source_id` — so the post-import `rebar validate`/`rebar fsck` step remains mandatory regardless of pre-check outcome.
evidence: 'Before invoking it, the operator confirms the target branch is current (git fetch + rebar exists <known-id>) against the intended tracker'
docs/concurrency.md I5: 'All writes go through the lock-guarded append+commit path'
src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py: idempotency scan runs at import start under the lock, making the pre-check a UX step not a safety invariant
The plan uses the word 'confirms' (implying a guarantee) for what is structurally a best-effort read-before-write check that a concurrent push invalidates
findings.finding: The plan frames 'git fetch + rebar exists <known-id>' as a safety confirmation that the target branch is current before invoking `rebar import`, but this is a check-then-act pattern that cannot exclude concurrent writes between the confirmation and rebar import's own write-lock acquisition. The plan's wording ('the operator confirms the target branch head before invocation') implies an ordering guarantee the pre-check cannot provide; the actual safety mechanisms are rebar import's own write lock (I5) and idempotency-by-source_id.
scenarios: Operator runs git fetch + rebar exists, sees expected state, then a collaborator pushes five new events; rebar import runs against a now-more-advanced branch — guidance implied this was prevented

Implementer of the guidance doc writes 'verify the branch is current before import to ensure safety', which is technically false and may suppress reporting of post-import fsck findings that would have caught problems
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'the operator confirms the target branch is current (git fetch + rebar exists <known-id>) before invocation' with: 'As a best-effort freshness check (not a safety invariant), run git fetch && rebar exists <known-id> to confirm the store is reachable and the expected ticket is present. Note: concurrent pushes between this check and rebar import are safe — rebar import's own write lock (I5) and idempotency-by-source_id are the actual safety mechanisms. The post-import rebar validate / rebar fsck step is mandatory regardless.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add one sentence of positive rationale to the Remediation section explaining why a separate docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md file (rather than inline AGENTS.md expansion) was chosen — e.g., anchoring the decision in AGENTS.md's 'keep this file lean' directive and the fact that ChatGPT does not natively load AGENTS.md at session start.
evidence: Absence: the Remediation section states what to add ('Add concise ChatGPT-specific guidance...') but not why a separate file (vs. inline AGENTS.md section, vs. a machine-readable capability manifest) is the right structural choice
AGENTS.md explicitly states 'Keep this file lean' — a positive rationale would anchor this choice in that existing constraint and make the decision self-documenting
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for choosing a separate guidance file (docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md) rather than alternative structural approaches, leaving the implementer without a stated basis for the architectural decision.
scenarios: A future maintainer wonders why ChatGPT guidance is separate from Codex guidance (both are in AGENTS.md) and has no stated principle to consult
A reviewer of the implementation cannot distinguish intentional scope from accidental omission without the rationale
findings.suggested_fix: Add after the opening sentence: 'A separate file is chosen because AGENTS.md must remain lean (it is @-imported at session start by other harnesses) and because ChatGPT sessions do not natively read AGENTS.md — a dedicated file can be linked explicitly when needed without bloating the canonical startup context.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct every citation of `schemas/export.schema.json` in the plan (Remediation 'Fallback payload schema', AC6 verify, AC7 verify) to the verified repository path `src/rebar/schemas/export.schema.json`.
evidence: Plan text (Remediation): 'fallback payload MUST conform to the rebar NDJSON export format (schemas/export.schema.json)'
Plan text (AC6): 'grep -qi export.schema.json docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md'
search_files('.', 'export.schema.json') returns only 'src/rebar/schemas/export.schema.json:3' — no root-level schemas/ path
list_directory('.') — confirmed: no 'schemas/' entry at repo root
File confirmed readable at src/rebar/schemas/export.schema.json
findings.finding: The plan's fallback payload schema reference points to `schemas/export.schema.json` (Remediation, AC6, AC7), but no `schemas/` directory exists at the repository root — the actual file is `src/rebar/schemas/export.schema.json`. A ChatGPT agent or implementer following the plan will reference a non-existent path when constructing the fallback payload or authoring the guidance.
findings.location: Remediation — 'Fallback payload schema' paragraph; AC6 verify step; AC7 verify step
scenarios: An implementer authors docs/chatgpt-agent-guide.md with the literal path 'schemas/export.schema.json'; agents reading that guide follow a broken reference and cannot locate the schema.
A ChatGPT session constructing a fallback payload tries to resolve 'schemas/export.schema.json' and fails, leaving the payload shape undefined.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `schemas/export.schema.json` with `src/rebar/schemas/export.schema.json` in each of the three plan locations (Remediation fallback paragraph, AC6, AC7).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the valid `ticket_type` values (`bug`, `epic`, `story`, `task`, `session_log`, `code_review`, `identity`) inline in the fallback-payload specification, or add a pointer to `src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json#/$defs/ticket_type` alongside the `export.schema.json` reference.
evidence: Plan text: 'containing at minimum: title, ticket_type, description…' — no enum values listed
src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json: 'ticket_type: { type: string, enum: [bug, epic, story, task, session_log, code_review, identity] }'
src/rebar/schemas/export.schema.json: allOf [$ref ticket_state.schema.json] — ticket_type is two $ref hops away from export.schema.json
Plan references only export.schema.json, not common.schema.json
findings.finding: The `ticket_type` field is required in the fallback payload (plan: 'at minimum: title, ticket_type, description…'), but the plan never enumerates valid `ticket_type` values nor points an agent to the `common.schema.json` where the enum is defined (`bug | epic | story | task | session_log | code_review | identity`). `export.schema.json` only `$ref`s `ticket_state.schema.json`, which itself `$ref`s `common.schema.json#/$defs/ticket_type` — a two-hop indirection not surfaced in the plan.
scenarios: A ChatGPT session generating a fallback payload for a new work item must pick a ticket_type value. With no enum in the plan, it may guess (e.g. 'feature') and produce an invalid payload that rebar import rejects.
The guidance document as authored may omit the valid type values, forcing agents to infer them from context.
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Fallback payload schema' paragraph, add: 'Valid ticket_type values (from src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json): bug | epic | story | task | session_log | code_review | identity.'

findings.finding: AC7 states the 'exceptional import mechanism accepts only native CLI-generated events, verifies the target branch head, appends new unique files, and runs tracker validation afterward' — but `rebar import` (the only existing import path) accepts NDJSON compiled-state projections (not 'native CLI-generated events'), assigns fresh local IDs with new HLC timestamps (not raw event files), and does not include a built-in tracker validation step afterward. The plan asserts this behavior either exists or will be straightforwardly achievable, but neither is substantiated.

AC7 mandates an 'exceptional import mechanism' with specific behaviors — accepting only native CLI-generated raw event files, verifying the target branch head, appending new unique files, and running tracker validation afterward — but no existing rebar CLI command or library function provides this surface. `rebar import` uses NDJSON projections with fresh local IDs (not raw event files) and does not include a branch-head verification step; direct raw-event-file appending outside the locked write path would violate I5 (single locked write path). The plan does not name which tool provides this capability, does not call for building one, and does not route the capability gap to a SPIKE before committing to documenting the pathway.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria item 7 / Remediation §'exceptional import path'
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) rewrite AC7 to specify that the import fallback uses `rebar export` / `rebar import` (the existing NDJSON path) with the ChatGPT session producing an export-compatible NDJSON payload, or (b) add a SPIKE task to design and build a branch-head-verifying raw-event import command before this AC is actionable.

findings.finding: AC7's exceptional import path is conditioned on 'if maintainers support one' in the Remediation section, leaving the executor unable to decide whether to write this section — it is a placeholder deferring a real design choice.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the conditional with a stated default: 'Document the exceptional import path (maintainers have confirmed this path is supported / this path is out of scope for this ticket and tracked in <child ticket id>).' Remove the 'if' so AC7 is unconditional.

findings.finding: AC7's 'exceptional import mechanism' (verify branch head, append new unique files, run tracker validation) may rebuild a pathway already provided by `rebar import`, which already composes events through the normal locked write path, is idempotent by source_id, and runs tracker validation implicitly — without the plan first evaluating whether that existing command satisfies the ChatGPT fallback scenario.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria item 7 / Remediation §'exceptional import path'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to AC7 (or a Remediation note): 'First evaluate `rebar import --dry-run` with a ChatGPT-prepared NDJSON export as the recovery vehicle; document the exceptional pathway only if `rebar import` cannot preserve the required invariants (e.g., original event UUIDs) for this use case.'

findings.finding: AC7's mechanism description ('appends new unique files') is ambiguous between the correct `rebar import` pathway (which routes through the single locked write path per I5) and a prohibited raw-file-injection pattern. The plan never names `rebar import` (or the locked write path) as the mechanism for the exceptional import path, leaving an implementer free to document direct filesystem writes to the tickets branch — which would violate I5 ('No side-channel writes') and undermine I2's filename-uniqueness guarantee, which is the CLI's responsibility.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC7: 'Any documented exceptional import mechanism accepts only native CLI-generated events, verifies the target branch head, appends new unique files, and runs tracker validation afterward.'
findings.suggested_fix: Revise AC7 to: 'Any documented exceptional import mechanism uses `rebar import` (or the native locked write path) — not raw event-file injection or Contents API writes — accepts only native CLI-generated NDJSON, verifies the target branch head before invocation, and runs `rebar fsck` or `rebar validate` afterward.'

findings.finding: AC6 requires the ChatGPT guidance to instruct sessions to produce 'reviewable ticket payloads' when native creation is impossible, but the plan never defines the schema, fields, or format of such a payload. Two candidate formats exist in the repo (the NDJSON export schema at schemas/export.schema.json referenced in docs/import-export.md, and the create_ticket MCP input shape in docs/mcp-reference.md), but neither is named. A session operating without CLI access must produce this payload from the guidance alone — an undefined-vocabulary schema an executor needs.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC6: 'The payload format is the rebar NDJSON export shape (`schemas/export.schema.json`), with at minimum the `ticket_type`, `title`, `status`, and `## Acceptance Criteria` description fields populated.' Alternatively reference the `create_ticket` MCP input schema if that is the intended review-and-import path.

findings.finding: The plan instructs the guidance to prohibit hand-authoring event files (AC5, citing the append-only reason) while also defining an exceptional import path that 'appends new unique files' (AC7). The append-only / I5 constraint ('all writes go through the lock-guarded append+commit path; No side-channel writes') is never co-located with the exceptional import protocol. This creates an instruction-locality gap: a session reading the guidance may not know whether the import path must route through the locked write path (`rebar import`) or whether direct file construction is permitted — the prohibition text and the permission text are structurally separated.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5 and AC7 (structural separation)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC7: 'The exceptional import path MUST use `rebar import` as the write vehicle (which routes through the single locked write path per I5); direct event-file construction or Contents API writes are explicitly prohibited even in this exceptional path.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
